commit.message:
fix(ci): explicitly import GPG key before Maven deploy

setup-java's gpg-private-key input can fail silently if the key
format doesn't match what it expects. Import it explicitly via
gpg --batch --import and pass the passphrase directly to mvn.

fix(java): use io.github.dever-labs groupId for Maven Central

io.mockly requires owning the mockly.io domain. Using the GitHub org
namespace io.github.dever-labs instead, which Maven Central verifies

commit.message:
ci: remove rust-publish job (crates.io requires overly broad GitHub OAuth)

crates.io only supports GitHub OAuth login and requests access to
private project boards, which is not acceptable. Rust users can
consume the client via git dependency instead:

  [dependencies]
  mockly-driver = { git = "https://github.com/dever-labs/mockly", tag = "v0.3.0" }

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(api): replace reslice pattern with make() to avoid memory retention

mocks[:0] reuses the underlying array, keeping deleted mock structs
reachable by the GC. Replace all 7 delete handlers with a fresh
make([]T, 0, len(mocks)) slice, each typed correctly for its protocol
(HTTP, WS, gRPC, GraphQL, TCP, Redis, MQTT).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(rust): assert free port is in unprivileged range (>= 1024)

Ports below 1024 are privileged on most OSes and would never be
returned by an OS-assigned (port 0) bind. Tighten the assertion to
match the .NET client test convention.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: mockly

commit.message:
fix(node): allocate both ports atomically to eliminate race condition

Introduce getFreePorts(n) which holds all n sockets open simultaneously
before releasing them together. This prevents another process from
claiming httpPort in the gap between the two sequential getFreePort()
calls, and guarantees the two ports are distinct.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: B24_APP_TOKEN required (not optional), add BITRIX_WEBHOOK_URL, fix schema commands

- B24_APP_TOKEN now marked as required in secrets table — registerBot() throws without it,
  botReply() downgrades to im.message.add v1 API without it
- Add BITRIX_WEBHOOK_URL to secrets table and all install sections
- Replace all 3 occurrences of --file schema.sql --remote with d1 migrations apply
- "Обновление схемы" section now uses migrations correctly

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(dotnet): resolve IDisposable resource leak findings

- PostAsync: add 'using' on StringContent so it is disposed after the
  HTTP request completes, preventing a socket/memory leak.
- GetFreePortAsync: replace TcpListener (underlying Socket not disposed)
  with 'using var socket = new Socket(...)' which is directly IDisposable.
  Also removes the spurious 'await Task.CompletedTask' — method is now sync
  wrapped in Task.FromResult.
- _process field: changed from Process to Process? so MocklyServer can be
  safely constructed with a null process (used in tests via reflection).
  StopAsync guards with 'if (_process is { HasExited: false })' and calls
  _process?.Dispose().
- Tests: GetFreePort_ReturnsValidPort now uses Socket with 'using'.
  All CreateTestServer-based tests use 'await using var server' so the
  HttpClient owned by MocklyServer is properly disposed after each test.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(dotnet): replace bare catch{} with specific exception types

Fixes 6 'generic catch clause' code scanning findings in MocklyServer.cs:

- Process.Kill() cleanup calls (3 sites): catch InvalidOperationException
  (process already exited) and Win32Exception (system-level error) instead
  of bare catch{} which swallows everything including CLR-fatal exceptions.
- StopAsync: same specific catches for Kill() + WaitForExitAsync().
- WaitReadyAsync: catch HttpRequestException and TaskCanceledException
  instead of bare catch{}, as these are the only expected 'not ready yet'
  exceptions from http.GetAsync().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: bundle mockly binary inside NuGet and npm packages

Previously both clients required downloading the binary at test time, breaking
offline CI, air-gapped environments, and adding complexity.

## NuGet (Mockly.Driver)
- Add build/Mockly.Driver.targets: MSBuild targets that copy the
  platform-appropriate binary to the output dir at build time (works for
  'dotnet test' without a full publish). Detects OS + architecture via
  MSBuild property functions.
- Add runtimes/{linux-x64,linux-arm64,osx-x64,osx-arm64,win-x64}/native/
  directory structure (populated by CI before pack).
- Update .csproj to include the targets file and runtimes/ binaries in the pack.
- Update MocklyInstaller.GetBinaryPath: check AppContext.BaseDirectory for
  the bundled binary (step 2) before falling back to binDir or download.

## npm (mockly-driver)
- Add platform sub-packages: mockly-driver-linux-x64, mockly-driver-linux-arm64,
  mockly-driver-darwin-x64, mockly-driver-darwin-arm64, mockly-driver-win32-x64.
  Each has os/cpu fields so npm only installs the matching one.
- Add optionalDependencies to package.json pointing at the platform packages.
- Update install.ts: getBundledBinaryPath() resolves the binary from the
  installed optional dep. getBinaryPath() and install() both check it before
  falling back to download.

## CI (ci.yml)
- Add nuget-publish job: stages binaries into runtimes/ structure, packs, and
  pushes to NuGet.org on release.
- Add npm-publish job: stages binaries into platform sub-packages, version-bumps
  all packages to match the release tag, publishes sub-packages then main package.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: mockly
repository.description: Cross-platform multi-protocol mock server — HTTP, WebSocket, gRPC, GraphQL, TCP, Redis, SMTP, MQTT — with a web UI, scenario system,  and fault injection.

commit.message:
fix(dotnet): replace Path.Combine with Path.Join to prevent silent argument dropping

Path.Combine silently drops earlier arguments when a later argument is an
absolute path. Path.Join (available since .NET Core 2.1) always concatenates
segments without this behaviour.

Fixes all 10 CA2249-style findings in MocklyInstaller.cs, MocklyServer.cs
and MocklyDriverTests.cs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Copilot/create first person sky app (#2)

* Initial plan

* feat: build complete Plane-POV first-person sky flight app

- Bootstrap React 18 + Vite project with Three.js/R3F stack
- Add 3D sky world with procedural clouds and Sky dome
- Add first-person flight controls (WASD + pointer lock)
- Add photo upload with IndexedDB persistence
- Add floating photo meshes with click-to-enlarge and likes
- Add HUD overlay, upload panel, and photo modal UI
- World radius expands based on number of uploaded photos
- Fix: persist likes to IndexedDB via upsert-all strategy
- Fix: add onerror handler in resizeImage to prevent stuck state
- Fix: hoist Vector2 to module constant in World.jsx
- Fix: guard setTimeout on unmount in UploadPanel
- Fix: suppress react-hooks/immutability for R3F camera pattern
- Build passes, lint exits 0

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/ArunachalamK2514/Plane-POV/sessions/690cd845-d826-4b48-8cc7-ad0822f76922

Co-authored-by: ArunachalamK2514 <76833422+ArunachalamK2514@users.noreply.github.com>

* Changed from react 18 to react 19

* Removed the sun and added a delete button to reset session storage

* fix: suppress motion eslint false-positive in ConfirmResetModal

The framer-motion `motion` namespace is used as JSX (<motion.div>) but
the no-unused-vars linter rule flags it as unused. Add the same
eslint-disable-line comment already present in UploadPanel and PhotoModal.

commit.message:
fix(quality): resolve all go vet 'using before error check' findings

Fix 9 instances in test files where http.Get/http.Post responses were
used via defer resp.Body.Close() before checking the error return.
If the HTTP call fails, resp is nil and the deferred close panics.

Replace each esp, _ := http.X(...) + defer resp.Body.Close()
with explicit error checks: esp, err := ...; if err != nil { t.Fatal }.

Files fixed:
- internal/protocols/httpserver/server_test.go (6 instances)
- internal/api/api_test.go (3 instances)

go vet: 0 issues, gosec: 0 issues, all tests passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(clients): correct Java models, deactivateScenario, and add POST /deactivate route

- Add POST /api/scenarios/{id}/deactivate alias alongside DELETE /activate
  so Go, Python, Rust, .NET, and Node.js clients all work consistently
- Fix Node.js deactivateScenario to call POST /deactivate (was DELETE /activate,
  correct but inconsistent with all other clients)
- Rewrite Java FaultConfig: type/probability/statusCode -> enabled/delay/status_override/error_rate
- Rewrite Java Scenario: description/mocks -> name/patches (matching actual API)
- Rewrite Java ScenarioPatch: addMocks/removeMockIds -> mock_id/status/body/delay
- Add scenarios field to MocklyConfig with builder methods
- Fix MocklyServer.writeConfig() to serialize scenarios as YAML
- Fix MocklyServer.toJson(FaultConfig) to use correct field names
- Update Java tests: toJsonFaultConfig, writeConfig reflection, MocklyConfig assertions,
  add writeConfigIncludesScenarios test

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(java): add port conflict, config, and HTTP API method tests

- Add isPortConflict() static helper to MocklyServer for detecting
  port-in-use error messages (address already in use, EADDRINUSE,
  bind: already in use)
- Add 6 reflection-based tests for isPortConflict covering true/false cases
- Add 2 reflection-based tests for writeConfig verifying file creation
  and YAML structure (ports, protocols, mockly sections)
- Add 9 tests for all HTTP API methods (addMock, deleteMock, reset,
  activateScenario, deactivateScenario, setFault, clearFault) using
  embedded com.sun.net.httpserver.HttpServer as a fake API server
- Request body is drained in the fake server handler to prevent
  connection-reset errors when the client sends a JSON body

Total: 31 tests (14 existing + 17 new), all passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve all gosec security and quality findings (37 → 0 issues)

HIGH severity (real bugs fixed):
- G115 (CWE-190): grpcserver — add safeGRPCCode() to prevent int→uint32
  overflow when converting mock error codes; codes outside 0-16 map to Unknown
- G112 (CWE-400): add ReadHeaderTimeout:5s to all 4 http.Server instances
  (httpserver, wsserver, graphqlserver, api/server) to prevent Slowloris attacks
- G705 (CWE-79): graphqlserver — use json.Marshal to safely encode
  OperationName in error response (was directly interpolated via %q)
- G306 (CWE-276): config.go — tighten WriteFile permissions from 0o644 to 0o600
- G404 (CWE-338): engine/funcs.go — replace global math/rand calls with a
  package-level *rand.Rand protected by a mutex + #nosec G404 annotation;
  mock data generation does not require crypto randomness

MEDIUM severity:
- G304 (CWE-22): config.go os.ReadFile — add #nosec G304 (intentional,
  user-supplied config path)
- G107 (CWE-88): cmd/mockly HTTP posts — add #nosec G107 (URL from trusted config)
- G404 (CWE-338): scenarios/store.go rand.New — add #nosec G404

LOW severity (unhandled errors):
- G104: tcpserver — handle ln.Close, conn.SetReadDeadline, conn.Write
- G104: redisserver — handle conn.Close
- G104: api/server — handle f.Close
- G104: cmd/mockly — handle fmt.Sscan (now returns error), tmpFile.Close
- G104: mqttserver — properly handle broker.Publish error (log + return)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: patient name autocomplete for new appointment

Replace Patient ID text input with patient name search + autocomplete.
Dropdown shows patient name, DOB, and phone for easy identification.
Selected patient's UUID is stored in hidden field. Falls back to
typed text if no patient selected (walk-in scenario).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: PDF shows patient info when checked in from appointment

Two fixes:
1. PDF endpoint: when patient_id isn't a UUID (e.g. 'zeyu'), search
   for the patient by name before falling back to walk-in mode.
   If a matching registered patient is found, use their full profile
   (name, DOB, phone, MRN, insurance).

2. Appointment check-in: when patient_id is a name (not UUID),
   search patients by name. If exactly one match, use their real
   patient_id for the visit, so the PDF will find their profile.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: multi-tenant data isolation via clinic_id

Migration: Added clinic_id TEXT column to all 14 Supabase tables
(patients, visits, staff, rooms, appointments, visit_treatments,
event_log, daily_reports, clinical_notes, insurance_policies,
documents, tasks, service_types, staff_service_types).

Backfill: All existing data assigned to Test Clinic
(0343c088-def0-457b-8043-af031c86fab1).

Service layer: All 48 Supabase query functions now filter by
clinic_id. All inserts include clinic_id. Events include clinic_id
in audit trail. Different clinics now see only their own data.

Indexes: Created on clinic_id for all high-traffic tables.

feat: clinic name in header + clickable user profile

   username, role, clinic name, clinic ID

as a separate migration task.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: template data generation functions

Add 18 new template functions for semi-random data generation in response bodies:

Random primitives:
  - {{uuid}}             — UUID v4 (crypto/rand)
  - {{rand_int 1 100}}   — integer in [min, max]
  - {{rand_float 0 1 2}} — float with N decimal places
  - {{rand_string 8}}    — alphanumeric string; optional charset:
                           alpha, lower, upper, numeric, hex, alphanumeric, or custom
  - {{rand_bool}}        — true / false

Selection:
  - {{pick "a" "b" "c"}} — randomly pick one of the given values

Fake structured data (no external dependency):
  - {{fake "name"}}      — full name (first + last)
  - {{fake "first_name"}} / {{fake "last_name"}}
  - {{fake "email"}}     — realistic email address
  - {{fake "username"}}  — username with random number suffix
  - {{fake "phone"}}     — +1-555-xxxx
  - {{fake "company"}}   — company name
  - {{fake "city"}} / {{fake "country"}} / {{fake "street"}} / {{fake "zip"}}
  - {{fake "ip"}} / {{fake "ipv6"}} / {{fake "url"}}
  - {{fake "useragent"}} / {{fake "word"}} / {{fake "sentence"}}

Sequences:
  - {{seq "counter"}}    — monotonic int per named counter (reset by /api/reset)

Date/time:
  - {{date "2006-01-02"}}        — formatted current date
  - {{date_add "2006-01-02" "24h"}} — date with duration offset

Text:
  - {{lorem 5}}            — N lorem ipsum words

Implementation:
  - internal/engine/funcs.go     — all generators + BuildFuncMap() + ResetSequences()
  - internal/engine/funcs_test.go — 25 tests covering all functions
  - internal/engine/matcher.go   — use BuildFuncMap() instead of inline FuncMap
  - internal/api/server.go       — call engine.ResetSequences() on /api/reset
  - README.md                    — full template reference table + example
  - configs/example.yaml         — create-user mock uses uuid, seq, fake, rand_int, now

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: event audit log with filters, table headers, date+time display

- Table with columns: Date & Time, Action, Who, Details
- Filters: date range, action type dropdown, text search
- Date defaults to today, shows MM/DD/YY HH:MM:SS format
- Action dropdown populated from actual event types
- Search filters across user name, patient name, event type

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: treatment records date filter uses local timezone + staff table headers

1. Treatment Records date filter now converts UTC check_in_time to
   America/New_York before comparing. Filtering 04/04-04/04 now shows
   visits that were at 11pm ET (stored as 3am UTC 04/05).

2. Ops Report staff table now has column headers: Name, Duration, Sessions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: update README and example config for component testing features

- Add response sequences, per-mock fault, query/body matching to features table
- Expand HTTP section with query, body_json, sequence, fault examples
- Add Component Testing section with workflow, call verification API, full example
- Add Call Verification API table to Management API Reference
- Remove stale duplicate content from README
- Update CI integration versions to v0.2.0
- Expand example.yaml with all new features + all 8 protocols + 3 scenarios

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: mockly
repository.description: Cross-platform multi-protocol mock server — HTTP, WebSocket, gRPC, GraphQL, TCP, Redis, SMTP, MQTT — with a web UI, scenario system,  and fault injection.

commit.message:
fix: report date filtering uses clinic local time (America/New_York)

check_in_time is stored in UTC. Visits at 11pm ET on 04/04 are stored
as 3am UTC 04/05, so startswith('2026-04-04') missed them entirely.
Now converts UTC to ET before date comparison, so 04/04 report shows
6 check-ins and Dr. Smith 90m matching the treatment records.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: component testing features - sequences, per-mock fault, query/body matching, call verification

- Response sequences with hold_last/loop/not_found exhaustion strategies
- Per-mock fault injection (status override, delay, error rate)
- Query parameter matching (exact or wildcard) in mock request config
- JSON body matching with dot-notation paths
- Call verification API (GET/DELETE /api/calls/http/{id}, POST wait)
- Call count tracking on HTTP server with reset support
- httpserver tests: sequences, per-mock fault, query, body, call counts
- engine matcher tests: query params, JSON body matching

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: mockly
repository.description: Cross-platform multi-protocol mock server — HTTP, WebSocket, gRPC, GraphQL, TCP, Redis, SMTP, MQTT — with a web UI, scenario system,  and fault injection.

commit.message:
fix: ops report date picker + staff hours from treatment durations

1. Added date picker to Ops Report so it can generate for any date,
   not just today. genReport() sends selected date to backend.

2. Staff hours now calculated from visit_treatments.duration_minutes
   (the session length set when adding treatments) instead of
   service_end_time - service_start_time which was just ~1 minute.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Kompasset sponsor badge to hero and fix post-match wording

- 'Sponsored by' + Kompasset logo in hero section below CTAs
- Logo links down to full sponsor section
- Changed 'post-match' to 'post-session' in sponsor blurb
- Reworded to remove match/win/loss language

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: report insert vs response separation, make actor_id optional

Separate DB insert (table columns only) from API response (enriched
with staff_hours and room_board_snapshot). Also make actor_id optional
in DailyReportGenerate schema since route uses current_user already.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
